content:
First note that the condition  
  p(5)+p(25)=1906  
for  
  p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d  
is equivalent to  
  15750a+650b+30c+2d = 1906  
⇔ 7875a+325b+15c+d = 953.  

Hence  
  d = 953 −(7875a+325b+15c).  

Now  
  p(15) = 3375a+225b+15c+d  
         = 3375a+225b+15c + 953 −(7875a+325b+15c)  
         = 953 −4500a −100b.  

Set m=45a+b.  Then  
  p(15)=953−100m,  
so  
  p(15)≡53 (mod 100).  

We want to make |p(15)| as small as possible among numbers ≡53 mod 100.  The two nearest to 0 are +53 and −47, and of these |−47|=47 is smaller.  One checks easily that m=10 gives 953−100·10=−47, which can be achieved by, say, choosing a=0, b=10, and any integers c,d satisfying the linear relation above.  

Therefore the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is 47.  

Answer:  \boxed{47}

First note that the condition  
  p(5)+p(25)=1906  
for  
  p(x)=ax³+bx²+cx+d  
is equivalent to  
  15750a+650b+30c+2d = 1906  
⇔ 7875a+325b+15c+d = 953.  

Hence  
  d = 953 −(7875a+325b+15c).  

Now  
  p(15) = 3375a+225b+15c+d  
         = 3375a+225b+15c + 953 −(7875a+325b+15c)  
         = 953 −4500a −100b.  

Set m=45a+b.  Then  
  p(15)=953−100m,  
so  
  p(15)≡53 (mod 100).  

We want to make |p(15)| as small as possible among numbers ≡53 mod 100.  The two nearest to 0 are +53 and −47, and of these |−47|=47 is smaller.  One checks easily that m=10 gives 953−100·10=−47, which can be achieved by, say, choosing a=0, b=10, and any integers c,d satisfying the linear relation above.  

Therefore the minimum possible value of |p(15)| is 47.  

Answer:  \boxed{47}
